ogTitle: Jackeys Marsh vs Jetsonville: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jackeys Marsh and Jetsonville.
heading: Jackeys Marsh vs Jetsonville — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jackeys Marsh, Tasmania and Jetsonville, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jetsonville has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Jackeys Marsh (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jackeys Marsh or Jetsonville safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jackeys Marsh has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Jetsonville scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jackeys Marsh scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Jetsonville scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jackeys Marsh or Jetsonville?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jetsonville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.38% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
